description: Manifest-bound schedule-aware analysis for Run 028 microSD-backed physical replay of the Run 027-style loose-threshold schedule.

b.role: loose usefulness-threshold schedule replayed from microSD

expected_headline.txa: TXA/N01: 16/16 schedule rows SEND; 378 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.539
expected_headline.txb: TXB/N16: 12/16 schedule rows SEND; 284 received packets; mean delivered usefulness 0.668
expected_headline.ratio: Observed received-packet ratio 0.7513; scheduled send-fraction ratio 0.7500.

interpretation: Run 028 replays the Run 027-style loose-threshold schedule from microSD rather than compiled firmware headers. TXA/N01 loaded a fixed-all 16/16 SEND schedule and TXB/N16 loaded a loose-threshold 12/16 SEND schedule from /schedule.csv. The observed TXB/TXA received-packet ratio was close to the scheduled send-fraction ratio, while TXB retained higher mean delivered usefulness per received packet than TXA. This supports a bounded storage-mechanism interpretation: the scheduled-skipping semantics can move from compiled headers to microSD-backed replay under similar two-transmitter lab conditions.
cautions: This is point-to-point LoRa at 915 MHz, not LoRaWAN.
The schedule CSVs define one repeated schedule period.
The analyzer compares schedule proportions and observed packet proportions.
The analyzer does not infer exact transmitted packet counts.
Missing sequence numbers are observed sequence gaps, not confirmed collisions.
recv_ms and tx_ms are not synchronized across boards and should not be interpreted as true latency.
Usefulness and priority are synthetic metadata in this milestone.
The run does not yet use a live belief-maintenance controller.
Use the wording reduced physical transmission attempts under scheduled skipping, not airtime optimization.
Do not claim energy savings unless current or power measurements are added.
Run 028 changes the storage and replay mechanism, not the underlying schedule semantics.
